_comment: Desired-state values for jarvis.toml. The config-drift-guard reads THIS file every 5 minutes and corrects any jarvis.toml value (and JARVIS__* User-scope ENV override) that drifts away from what is pinned here. This file is the moving source of truth: the guard only READS it, it never writes it. IMPORTANT (changed 2026-05-28): brain.primary is now ALSO written automatically by the desktop app's provider switch (the API/Settings section in the UI). When you switch the active Brain provider in the UI, the app writes all three layers at once -- jarvis.toml [brain].primary, this config-soll.json brain.primary, and the JARVIS__BRAIN__PRIMARY User-scope ENV var -- so all three agree on the new value and the drift-guard sees NO drift and will NOT revert your choice. Editing brain.primary here by hand still works, but the UI provider switch is the normal way to change the active provider. The drift-guard's job is to PROTECT whatever value is set here against rogue parallel sessions (which only touch jarvis.toml, not this file). Keys use dot-notation, exactly as the TOML section header reads. Paths in Punkt-Notation, exakt wie in der TOML.
_reason: 2026-05-28: sub-agent heavy-worker LLM upgraded Opus 4.7 -> 4.8 (current Anthropic frontier; both verified live under Claude Max OAuth). Pinned brain.providers.claude-api.deep_model=claude-opus-4-8 here so the drift-guard enforces it (it was previously unpinned). 2026-05-28: brain.primary is now ALSO written by the desktop-app UI provider switch as a three-layer sync (jarvis.toml [brain].primary + this config-soll.json brain.primary + the JARVIS__BRAIN__PRIMARY User-scope env var) so the drift-guard sees no drift and never reverts a deliberate switch; the guard was hardened to be a true no-op when all three layers already agree, and a latent PowerShell foreach scope-leak that left the ENV-drift check inert was fixed. 2026-05-27: Cartesia.ai Sonic 3.5 added as a selectable TTS provider with per-language native voice mapping (Sebastian/Daniel/Pedro for DE/EN/ES) synced to the reply_language pin + per-utterance auto-detect. Earlier 2026-05-26 (Vertex AI): tts.use_vertex + vertex_project + vertex_location + service_account_path added — routes Gemini Flash TTS through Vertex AI on the user's billed GCP project instead of Google AI Studio. AI Studio enforces a 100-RPD cap on the Preview model gemini-3.1-flash-tts-preview that is independent of Pay-as-you-go billing; once hit, the Sibling-Bridge auto-switches to gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts and the perceived Charon voice shifts mid-session, breaking the 2026-05-24 single-voice user mandate. Vertex AI on a billed project does not have the Preview RPD cap. Auth runs via a service-account JSON at ~/.config/jarvis/vertex-sa.json (dedicated SA, role roles/aiplatform.user, key on disk). The plugin strips GOOGLE_API_KEY + GEMINI_API_KEY at client-build time so the AI-Studio path cannot be accidentally re-routed when both auth modes are present. The Sibling-Bridge stays wired as defence-in-depth but will not trigger on Vertex. Earlier 2026-05-26: stt.bias_prompt added — Whisper-prompt vocabulary biasing for the Groq cloud STT plugin. Without it, Whisper falls onto phonetically nearby everyday words for proper nouns and tech vocabulary. The prompt is a comma-separated word list (NOT full sentences — Whisper would re-emit those verbatim on silent tail audio) and is capped to 1024 chars inside the plugin. Earlier 2026-05-26: computer_use.show_virtual_cursor pinned to false after the black-screen incident. Earlier 2026-05-24: trigger.single_turn_mode switched true -> false (USER mandate). EARLIER 2026-05-20: brain.sub_jarvis.provider switched from chatgpt back to claude-api. Earlier 2026-05-20: main Jarvis voice brain switched to Gemini 3.5 Flash (GA, model id `gemini-3.5-flash`, 1M-token context). Earlier 2026-05-18: voice single-turn mandate. Earlier 2026-05-15: all brain layers on Gemini.

stt.bias_prompt: Jarvis, OpenClaw, Codex, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Whisper, Wispr Flow, Subagent, Sub-Agent, Mission-Manager, Critic-Loop, Critic, Worker, Router-Brain, Router, Kontrollierer, Voice-Pipeline, Wake-Word, Silero, VAD, TTS, STT, Telemetry, Transkript, Token, Provider, Plugin, Sprechblase, Orb, Mascot, Quip, Browser, Terminal, Repository, Branch, Commit, Push, Pull-Request, Issue, Merge, Refactor, Deployment, Screenshot, Debuggen, Workflow, Microservice, Container, Docker, Kubernetes, FastAPI, React, TypeScript, Python, JSON, Markdown, Logfile, Konfiguration, Endpoint, Webhook, API-Key, Service-Account, Cloud, Google Cloud, Vertex AI, BigQuery, GitHub, Twilio, ElevenLabs, OAuth, Credential Manager, Frontend, Backend, Skript, Daemon, Hangup, Auflegen, Denkpause, submitted, distanziert, erkennt
